name: Neuchâtel (Switzerland)
description: A square flag of three vertical bands, green, white and red, with a small white couped cross in the upper fly.
history: Unrelated to the older flag of the town of Neuchâtel, this is the banner of the 1848 republican revolution; it became the official cantonal flag in 1857 after Prussia renounced its lingering claim to Neuchâtel.

features.symbolism: the green, white and red of the 1848 republican movement

features.role: small white couped cross in the upper fly
